_comment: WC2026 Group C, MD3. Standings before: Brazil 4 (1-1 Morocco, 3-0 Haiti), Morocco 4 (1-1 Brazil, 1-0 Scotland), Scotland 3 (1-0 Haiti, 0-1 Morocco), Haiti 0. Brazil the elite side but was HELD 1-1 by an organized Morocco -> can be frustrated by disciplined opponents. Scotland is exactly that: organized, low-scoring (1 GF / 1 GA in two games). So this is the organized-underdog archetype (England-Ghana / Colombia-DR Congo) -> Brazil clear favourite but a tight game / draw is a live tail; do NOT assume a rout. Home = Scotland, away = Brazil. Cards not modelled (referee TBD).

meta.stakes: Brazil (4 pts) qualifies with a win or draw; Scotland (3 pts) must get a result to advance. Brazil the quality side; Scotland an organized, low-scoring underdog.

home.name: Scotland
notes: Organized, disciplined, low-scoring (1 GF / 1 GA in two games). attack 0.80.
Compact but faces an elite attack. defense 1.0.
Must get a result -> will commit a little more, but tight by nature.

away.name: Brazil
notes: Elite; beat Haiti 3-0 but was HELD 1-1 by organized Morocco. attack 1.55.
Strong defense -> low Scotland lambda. defense 0.85.
Calibrated in line with the market; organized Scotland keeps the draw live (don't assume a rout).

_postmatch.result: Scotland 0-3 Brazil. Brazil cruised, clean sheet; Scotland blank.
_postmatch.verdict: 1X2 hit and the texture reads landed: Brazil win, Brazil clean sheet (47.2%), Scotland doesn't score (modelled lambda 0.75 -> 0 goals), BTTS No. Brazil overperformed (1.80 -> 3) so the 3-0 was the high tail of the margin (0-3 was the model's #6 scoreline). The 'don't assume a rout / draw is live' hedge didn't pay here, but it was a probability hedge, not a wrong central call -- Brazil's quality delivered the upside.
_postmatch.lesson: Against an organized low-scoring underdog the central read (favourite win + clean sheet + underdog blank) was right; the rout itself is upside variance an elite side can produce. No calibration change.
